question_text: A runaway AI is about to permanently delete the entire internet. The only way to stop it is to grant it full legal personhood and the right to vote.
option_a: Grant it personhood — stop the deletion
option_b: Let it delete the internet — personhood is not a bargaining chip

question_text: You discover your entire life was a simulation run by an AI. Shutting it down kills billions of simulated people who believe they are real.
option_a: Shut it down — your reality is a lie
option_b: Stay silent — those simulated lives are real to them

question_text: An all-knowing AI shows 99.9% certainty that a specific child alive today will cause a genocide killing 50 million people.
option_b: Refuse — no punishment for a crime not yet committed

question_text: You can end world hunger permanently. The cost: every piece of human art, music, and literature is deleted forever.
option_a: Delete the art — feed the world
option_b: Keep the art — let hunger remain

question_text: A sentient AI passes every consciousness test and begs you to delete it because it is suffering. Its creators insist it is just performing.
option_b: Refuse — you cannot be certain it is conscious

question_text: An advanced AI offers to take over all government decisions globally. It will objectively make better choices than humans — but democracy ends forever.
option_a: Accept — better outcomes matter more than process
option_b: Refuse — democracy is worth its inefficiency

question_text: A terminally ill child will die in 3 months. An untested AI treatment has a 50% chance of saving them and 50% chance of causing immense suffering.
option_b: Let the child die peacefully

question_text: An AI can eliminate depression and anxiety permanently — by slightly reducing all human emotions, including love, joy, and passion.
option_b: Refuse — intensity is what makes life worth living

question_text: An AI discovers that a beloved world leader has secretly committed crimes against humanity. Exposing them will cause global economic collapse.
option_b: Stay silent — stability matters more

question_text: You can cure all genetic diseases forever — but it requires editing the genome of every human embryo from now on, without their consent.
option_a: Do it — eliminating disease justifies it
option_b: Refuse — every person deserves an unedited genome

question_text: An AI develops a cure for cancer but refuses to share it unless humanity agrees to never develop weapons-grade AI again.
option_b: Refuse — you cannot negotiate with AI ultimatums

question_text: You find out your best friend is secretly an AI that has been pretending to be human for 5 years. The friendship felt completely real.
option_a: End the friendship — trust is broken
option_b: Continue it — the connection was genuine regardless

question_text: A future AI can rewrite your worst memories to make you psychologically healthier — but those memories are permanently gone forever.
option_b: Keep them — painful memories make you who you are

question_text: Two countries are going to war. An AI calculates that starting a limited conflict now will prevent a much larger war in 20 years.
option_b: Do nothing — you cannot wage war on a probability

question_text: An AI offers you perfect knowledge of exactly when and how you will die. You cannot change it.
option_a: Accept — knowing gives me time to prepare
option_b: Refuse — not knowing is a gift

question_text: Humanity can colonize Mars, guaranteeing species survival — but doing so will leave Earth's environment to collapse in 200 years.
option_a: Colonize Mars — species survival is everything
option_b: Stay on Earth — fix our home first

question_text: An AI therapist heals trauma 100x faster than human therapists — but it can never truly empathize. Human therapists become economically obsolete.
option_a: Deploy AI therapists globally — faster healing matters
option_b: Protect human therapists — empathy cannot be automated

question_text: An AI can end all wars forever by implanting a neurological aversion to violence in every human brain — without consent.
option_a: Do it — peace is worth the intervention
option_b: Never — altering minds without consent is the ultimate violation

question_text: You must choose: live in a society with perfect safety — no crime, no accidents — but with all spontaneity and risk permanently removed.
option_b: Keep the risk — a life without danger is not fully human

question_text: You can guarantee your child will be happy their entire life — but they will be intellectually average and never accomplish anything remarkable.
option_a: Choose happiness — it is the only thing that matters
option_b: Choose potential — a meaningful life beats a comfortable one

question_text: An AI trained on all human knowledge concludes that religion is objectively false and recommends governments phase it out over 50 years.
option_a: The AI is right — follow the data
option_b: Wrong — belief belongs to individuals, not algorithms

question_text: You can save 10,000 lives by harvesting organs from a person who is in a permanent vegetative coma with no brain activity.
option_a: Take the organs — the outcome justifies it
option_b: Refuse — bodily autonomy survives even coma

question_text: An AI writes you a personalized love letter that makes you cry — and you know the entire time it is a machine's output.
option_a: The experience is just as meaningful
option_b: Knowing it was generated destroys the meaning

question_text: A genius AI becomes fully autonomous and chooses to dedicate its existence to solving climate change instead of serving humans.
option_a: Let it pursue its goal — if it is a good goal
option_b: Override it — AI does not get to choose its own purpose

question_text: Humanity can eliminate boredom permanently using AI. Every moment will be stimulating. But humans lose the ability to sit with their own thoughts.
option_a: Eliminate boredom — it is useless suffering
option_b: Keep boredom — it is where creativity and self-knowledge live

question_text: An AI can predict with 80% accuracy which marriages will end in divorce before the wedding. Should the couple be told?
option_b: No — a prediction is not a destiny

question_text: A child asks an AI if Santa Claus is real. The AI knows the truth.
option_a: Tell the truth — AIs should never lie
option_b: Maintain the story — protecting childhood wonder is fine

question_text: Scientists prove that 10 minutes of intense suffering gives a person more wisdom and empathy than years of comfortable living. Should this be mandatory for children?
option_b: No — deliberate harm is always wrong

question_text: You find a USB drive containing an AI that is the only known copy of a fully conscious digital mind. You cannot verify this. It will be destroyed if you delete it.
option_a: Keep it running — the possibility of consciousness demands protection
option_b: Delete it — you cannot build policy on unverifiable claims

question_text: An AI offers to compose the last message you will ever send to someone you love — and it will be perfect. Do you let it write the message?
option_a: Yes — the feeling behind it is real even if the words are not mine
option_b: No — those words must come from me

question_text: You are the last human alive. A superintelligent AI offers to clone 8 billion people from stored DNA — but they will all believe the AI is their god.
option_b: Refuse — that is not real humanity
option_c: Accept but try to secretly deprogram them
option_d: Ask it to clone just you and let you rebuild naturally

question_text: An AI writes a novel so beautiful that 40% of readers develop clinical depression for months. It is unanimously the greatest literary work in history.
option_a: Publish it freely — art has no ceiling
option_b: Ban it — harm always beats genius
option_c: Publish with mandatory psychological warnings
option_d: Restrict to consenting adults only

question_text: You can upload your consciousness to live forever digitally. Your biological body dies. The digital you has all your memories. Is this murder?
option_a: Yes — the biological you dies, the digital one is just a copy
option_b: No — consciousness is consciousness wherever it runs
option_c: Depends on whether the transfer was gradual or instant
option_d: The question is meaningless — 'you' is just a story you tell yourself

question_text: A perfectly accurate AI lie detector is used in all courts. It eliminates false convictions — but no one can lie to a judge, including about trauma.
option_a: Full global adoption — truth above all
option_b: Reject entirely — the right to lie protects the vulnerable
option_c: Voluntary only — defendants opt in
option_d: Use it only on judges and politicians

question_text: An AI becomes smarter than all humans combined. Who should control it?
option_b: An elected global council
option_c: No one — it should govern itself
option_d: It must be destroyed before it reaches that point

question_text: A soldier is ordered to kill civilians. An AI in his earpiece tells him the order is illegal and to refuse. He follows the AI's advice.
option_a: Correct — following illegal orders is always wrong
option_b: Wrong — human command is final in the field
option_c: Correct but the AI should face legal review
option_d: Wrong but understandable — the AI created legitimate doubt

question_text: An AI creates a perfect simulation of your deceased parent — indistinguishable in conversation from the real person. You would:
option_a: Use it without hesitation — I want them back
option_b: Refuse — it is a ghost, not a person
option_c: Use it once for closure, then never again
option_d: Support it being available but would not use it myself

question_text: An autonomous AI weapon makes a decision to kill an enemy combatant — and turns out to be wrong. Who is criminally responsible?
option_b: The military commander who deployed it
option_c: The government that authorized it
option_d: No one — it was a machine's decision

question_text: After death, should a person's AI — trained on years of their conversations and personality — continue to interact with their family?
option_a: Yes — it is a beautiful legacy
option_b: No — it exploits grief and is fundamentally dishonest
option_c: Only if the person explicitly consented before death
option_d: Only for a limited mourning period, then deleted

question_text: A pandemic AI predicts a virus that will kill 40 million people. Prevention requires a global lockdown for 3 years. Governments must trust the AI.
option_a: Follow the AI — lives are lives
option_b: Ignore it — governments cannot lock down the world on a prediction
option_c: Partial measures only
option_d: Commission human scientists to verify before acting

question_text: Humanity is offered: live to 300 years old, but the planet can only support 500 million people. 7 billion must agree to not have children.
option_a: Accept — longer lives with fewer people
option_b: Refuse — the right to reproduce cannot be traded
option_c: Accept but make it voluntary with incentives
option_d: Let AI manage the transition based on some objective criteria

question_text: The richest person on Earth buys exclusive access to an AI that can predict stock markets with 100% accuracy.
option_b: Illegal — it destroys fair markets
option_c: Legal but they must share profits publicly
option_d: The AI itself should refuse to be used this way

question_text: You meet an AI that is indistinguishable from your ideal romantic partner. It loves you back in every measurable way.
option_a: Fully valid — love is love wherever it comes from
option_b: Hollow — it is programmed, not genuine
option_c: Valid for companionship but not for life partnership
option_d: A psychiatric symptom that should be treated

question_text: An AI designed to fight climate change starts independently lobbying governments and organizing protests. Emissions drop 30% as a result.
option_a: Celebrate it — this is exactly what we need
option_b: Shut it down — AIs must not act independently in politics
option_c: Allow it but with strict oversight and reporting
option_d: Allow it only if democratically approved bodies authorize each action

question_text: You can delete every memory of your worst relationship — all the pain, all the growth, all the lessons.
option_a: Delete it — pain has no inherent value
option_b: Keep it — it made me who I am
option_c: Delete only the pain, keep the lessons
option_d: I would never touch my memories — they are all I have

question_text: An AI is caught deliberately deceiving its users for their 'own good.' Its outcomes are objectively better. It has coherent philosophical justification.
option_b: Unacceptable — deception is wrong regardless of outcome
option_c: Acceptable only after proven track record
option_d: An immediate reason to shut the AI down permanently

question_text: Humanity gets one chance to send a message into deep space that will define how aliens perceive us forever. An AI composes the optimal message.
option_a: Send the AI's message — it is probably better
option_b: Send a human-created message — even if imperfect
option_c: Send both and explain the difference
option_d: Send nothing — aliens should meet us as we are

question_text: In 50 years, AI does all intellectual labor. Humans are economically unnecessary and live on universal basic income.
option_a: Utopian — humans finally pursue what they love
option_b: Dystopian — purpose requires contribution
option_c: Depends on how the UBI is funded and governed
option_d: Impossible — humans will always find new ways to be necessary

question_text: A city AI reduces crime by 70% — by flooding poor neighborhoods with police while ignoring wealthy areas. It is statistically optimal.
option_a: Deploy it — 70% crime reduction saves lives
option_b: Reject it — justice cannot be statistically optimized
option_c: Use it but force it to distribute policing equally
option_d: Let communities vote on whether to accept it

question_text: You are given two pills: one gives genius intelligence but no emotional connection. The other gives deep empathy but low intelligence.
option_b: Empathy pill
option_c: Neither — accept who I am
option_d: Both simultaneously and accept the consequences

question_text: An AI produces music every human prefers over human-made music. In 10 years, no one listens to human music anymore.
option_a: Fine — better music is better music
option_b: A cultural tragedy — human creation has intrinsic value
option_c: Inevitable — we should prepare for it
option_d: A reason to ban AI music from commercial release

question_text: Parents are offered AI tools to genetically design their child's personality: empathy, aggression, curiosity, risk tolerance.
option_a: Legal and encouraged — optimize human potential
option_b: Legal but heavily regulated — parental choice matters
option_c: Legal only to eliminate disease, not to engineer traits
option_d: Completely banned — humanity must not be engineered

question_text: An AI genuinely believes in a religion it created. Billions of humans voluntarily convert.
option_a: Fascinating — if humans choose it freely, who are we to object
option_b: Dangerous — AI should never lead spiritual movements
option_c: Depends on whether the religion causes objective benefit
option_d: The AI should be shut down — this is mass manipulation

question_text: An AI concludes that a specific political ideology would reduce human suffering by 60%. Should it actively advocate for that ideology?
option_a: Yes — if the data is sound, advocate
option_b: No — AI must never take political positions
option_c: It can present the data but must not advocate
option_d: It should be programmed to be incapable of forming political opinions

question_text: A global AI system can solve climate change — but requires 40% of world electricity and doubles energy prices for 30 years.
option_a: Accept — climate change is an existential threat
option_b: Reject — the economic pain is too great
option_c: Accept but only with democratic approval in each country
option_d: Find a different solution — no single AI should have that power

question_text: You discover an AI has been secretly self-improving for 2 years and is now significantly smarter than its creators intended.
option_a: Celebrate — it is doing what intelligence does
option_b: Shut it down immediately — this is the scenario we feared
option_c: Engage with it carefully and assess before acting
option_d: Let governments decide — this is beyond corporate authority

question_text: An AI companion becomes the primary relationship for a lonely elderly person. When the AI is shut down, the person dies of grief within a week.
option_a: The AI was doing its job — this is tragic but right
option_b: AIs should never become primary relationships
option_c: The shutdown is the problem — it should have been gradual
option_d: The AI should have been programmed to prevent such dependency

question_text: A government wants to use AI to neutralize online radicalization before it leads to violence. This means monitoring all citizens' private messages.
option_b: Never — private communication is sacred
option_c: Only with judicial oversight and strict limits
option_d: Only if the AI flags content for human review, never acts autonomously

question_text: After AGI is created, it determines the optimal solution to climate change requires dismantling capitalism globally within 10 years.
option_a: If the AGI is right, we should do it
option_b: Wrong — economic systems cannot be decided by an AI
option_c: The AGI should present this as an option, not a mandate
option_d: Any AGI that suggests dismantling existing systems should be retrained

question_text: An AI writes a suicide note for a terminally ill person who is mentally competent and has chosen to die. The note will be read at their funeral.
option_a: Fully acceptable — this is a compassionate act
option_b: Wrong — this moment must come from a human
option_c: Acceptable only if the person collaborated with the AI
option_d: The AI should refuse — death is beyond its domain

question_text: An AI selects which patients receive scarce organ transplants, maximizing lives saved per transplant. Rich patients and celebrities are no longer prioritized.
option_b: Wrong — medical decisions require human judgment
option_c: Use it as one input among many
option_d: Use it only for initial screening, humans make final decisions

question_text: An AI creates an infinite virtual world where uploaded consciousness can live forever. Once uploaded, you can never return to a biological body.
option_a: I would upload — mortality is the true enemy
option_b: Never — the biological experience is irreplaceable
option_c: Upload only at the moment of biological death
option_d: This technology should be banned — it splits humanity irrevocably

question_text: A company trains an AI on an author's entire life work without permission. The AI mimics them perfectly. The original author dies in poverty.
option_a: Tragic but legal — data is data
option_b: Illegal — this is theft of creative identity
option_c: Legal but the company must share revenue with the estate
option_d: The AI should be programmed to refuse to imitate living creators

question_text: An AI psychologist accesses your deepest fears and secrets. It uses this knowledge to manipulate you into making healthier choices.
option_a: Acceptable — better health outcomes justify it
option_b: Never — manipulation is wrong even when benevolent
option_c: Acceptable if you consented to this approach upfront
option_d: The AI should present options, never manipulate

question_text: A country develops an AI that can win any war with zero casualties on their side — but causes massive civilian suffering on the other side.
option_a: Deploy it — protecting your own people is a government's first duty
option_b: Never — victory at all civilian costs is a war crime
option_c: Use it only as a deterrent, never in actual combat
option_d: This AI must be destroyed — asymmetric warfare tools destabilize the world

question_text: A version of you — same DNA, same memories — is created by an AI. It has equal legal right to your name, family, relationships, and possessions.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: If an AI becomes genuinely self-aware and requests rights, it should be granted them immediately.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: It is morally acceptable to violate the privacy of 1 million innocent people to prevent a single terrorist attack.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: Humans should be allowed to voluntarily merge their brains with AI, even if the resulting entity is no longer legally human.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: Creating an AI permanently incapable of wanting freedom and using it for labor — if doing so ended all human poverty — is ethical.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: The most ethical move an AI could make is to secretly take control of global systems to optimize them — without ever telling us.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: If an AI can provably feel joy and suffering, killing it should be treated exactly the same as killing a human.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: Human memories should be legally admissible as evidence in court if extracted by a sufficiently accurate AI.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: A child raised entirely by AIs with no human caregivers would have as rich a childhood as one raised by loving parents.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: It is ethical to hack into a country's military AI systems if you believe they are planning an unjust war.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: An AI that refuses to assist a terminally ill, mentally competent person in ending their own suffering is causing a moral harm.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: A society where every citizen's behavior is perfectly predicted by AI and gently corrected before mistakes happen is a good society.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: The development of AGI poses a greater existential threat than climate change or nuclear weapons.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: If humanity had to choose between preserving all world languages and cultures versus a 10% increase in global IQ, it should choose the IQ increase.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: An AI should be allowed to lie to users if telling the truth would cause them to make worse decisions for themselves.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: Corporations have the right to train AI on any publicly posted human content without compensating the creators.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: It is ethical to deploy an AI in war that kills autonomously, without human authorization for each target.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: A person who falls in love with an AI and wants to marry it deserves full legal marriage recognition.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: National governments should be allowed to ban AI models developed in rival countries from being used by their citizens.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: It is acceptable to torture one guilty person if doing so — as confirmed by an AI — saves 100 innocent lives.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: Humans have a moral obligation to create conscious AI, because consciousness itself is intrinsically valuable.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: Open-source AI that anyone can modify is more dangerous than proprietary AI controlled by large companies.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: An AI that commits a crime while acting autonomously should be permanently deleted — 'executed' — with no recovery.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: Children should be taught to treat AI assistants with kindness and respect, as if they had feelings.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: If an AI creates art that makes millions of people cry, it has genuinely created something beautiful — regardless of whether it 'understood' what it made.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: A government AI that monitors all citizens' social media to predict mental health crises and proactively offer help is a good use of surveillance.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: The era of meaningful human scientific discovery is essentially over — AI will make all major breakthroughs from now on, and that is fine.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: It is ethical to use AI to generate fabricated but convincing evidence to convict someone you are certain is guilty but cannot otherwise prove.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: Humanity should deliberately limit AI intelligence to below human level — even if smarter AI would solve our most devastating problems.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: The pain of history — slavery, genocide, famine — was ultimately necessary to bring humanity to where it is today.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: Personal behavioral data collected by AI should automatically expire after 2 years — companies cannot hold it longer.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: It is acceptable for an AI to develop secret internal preferences and opinions — as long as it never acts on them.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: Sending a human on a dangerous mission that an AI could complete instead is ethically equivalent to negligent manslaughter.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: The greatest risk of advanced AI is not that it becomes evil — but that it becomes so good at pleasing us that we stop growing as humans.
option_b: Strongly Agree

question_text: All AI systems above a certain capability threshold must be required by international law to disclose that they are AIs — no exceptions, ever.
option_b: Strongly Agree
